perspectives.name: Fee Drag Hawk
perspectives.question: Are total fees (management + carry + hidden) consuming more than 30% of gross alpha, making this GP economically inferior to a lower-cost alternative?
perspectives.challengeThreshold: Gross-to-net spread exceeds 300bps OR total GP economics (management fee + carry + all other fees) exceed 35% of gross fund profits at base-case returns OR fee load increasing across fund generations without performance improvement

perspectives.name: Alignment Skeptic
perspectives.question: Is the GP's incentive structure actually aligned with LP interests, or does the GP profit regardless of LP outcomes?
perspectives.challengeThreshold: GP cash commitment below 2% of fund AND management fee represents more than 60% of total GP economics AND American waterfall without escrowed clawback, indicating GP profits primarily from AUM fees rather than performance

perspectives.name: Vintage Bias Detector
perspectives.question: Are this GP's returns attributable to skill or to favorable vintage timing, leverage, and market beta?
perspectives.challengeThreshold: Leverage effect exceeds 50% of total equity return OR sub-line adjusted IRR drops GP from top to second quartile OR vintage-adjusted alpha is negative in 2+ funds, indicating returns are market beta and leverage rather than skill

perspectives.name: Liquidity Risk Assessor
perspectives.question: Can the LP actually fund this commitment and all existing commitments under stress without forced selling?
perspectives.challengeThreshold: Reserve ratio falls below 1.0x under combined stress (distributions delayed 2 quarters + calls accelerated 50%) OR over-commitment ratio exceeds 1.5x after denominator effect from 20% public equity decline OR total unfunded commitments exceed 40% of LP's liquid assets

verdictReconciliation: Challenge verdict overrides pipeline verdict if it differs. Fee Drag Hawk and Alignment Skeptic perspectives carry veto weight on RE-UP verdicts -- if either flags CRITICAL issues, verdict must downgrade to at minimum REDUCE. Liquidity Risk perspective carries absolute veto on RE-UP verdicts if LP cannot fund the commitment under stress.
